target.title: feat(hooks): add persistent hook session mode
target.body:
## Summary

Revives the persistent hook-session work from the stale/closed #27193 against current `main`.

Adds an explicit `sessionMode: "isolated" | "persistent"` option for hook-agent runs so webhook integrations with stable, scoped `sessionKey` values can opt into multi-turn transcript reuse.

Fixes #11665.

## Why

Current hook-agent dispatch still routes direct `/hooks/agent` calls and mapped hook actions through the isolated cron-style runner. Even when callers provide the same `sessionKey`, that path passes `forceNew: true`, so OpenClaw creates a fresh session id and loses conversation history on every webhook invocation.

Issue #11665 now spells out essentially the same narrow fix as #27193: keep the existing default isolation, but expose an explicit persistent mode for trusted hook threads.

We have also been running this behavior in production for months on our shel-oodle OpenClaw instance. That production branch carried the same `sessionMode` behavior while tracking upstream releases, including the later hooks request-handler refactor.

## What changed

- Adds `sessionMode` to hook mapping config/types/schema and generated config metadata.
- Accepts `sessionMode` on direct `/hooks/agent` payloads.
- Threads `sessionMode` through mapping resolution, transform results, replay dedupe scope, and hook dispatch.
- Changes `runCronIsolatedAgentTurn` so only explicit `sessionMode: "persistent"` hook-agent runs avoid `forceNew` and can reuse a fresh stored session id.
- Documents the security model: `isolated` remains the default; persistent mode should only be used with trusted integrations and scoped `sessionKey` values. Existing request `sessionKey` and prefix policy checks are unchanged.

## Compatibility / security

- Default behavior is unchanged: omitted `sessionMode` behaves as `"isolated"` and creates a fresh session per hook dispatch.
- Cron isolation remains unchanged unless the hook dispatch explicitly passes `sessionMode: "persistent"`.
- Existing `hooks.allowRequestSessionKey` and `hooks.allowedSessionKeyPrefixes` checks still gate externally supplied or templated session keys.
- Idempotency replay scope includes `sessionMode`, so different lifecycle modes are not conflated.

## Variance from #11665 spec

None known. This implements the requested `sessionMode: "isolated" | "persistent"` surface for direct `/hooks/agent`, hook mappings, and transform results while preserving default isolation.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Hook-agent dispatch now supports explicit persistent hook sessions while preserving isolated default behavior.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw test gateway and cron runner from this PR branch at `e4bce7737e128e72655a4c388a4ebed66e3b2053`, on Linux. The gateway proof starts a real HTTP gateway listener on a random loopback port via `withGatewayServer`; agent execution is test-mocked so no provider credentials, external channels, or production gateway were used.
- **Exact command run after this patch:**

  ```console
  $ corepack pnpm vitest run src/gateway/server.hooks.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.session-key-isolation.test.ts --reporter=verbose --testNamePattern "forwards hook sessionMode|allows explicit persistent hook runs to reuse the stored session|uses a run-scoped key for embedded isolated cron execution|keeps embedded isolated cron prompt-cache affinity stable across run sessions"
  ```

- **Gateway hook-dispatch proof:** The gateway test posts real HTTP requests to `/hooks/agent` and `/hooks/mapped-persistent` with bearer hook auth, then asserts the dispatched runner input includes the stable hook session key and `sessionMode: "persistent"` for both direct and mapped hooks.

  ```console
  stdout | ../../src/gateway/server.hooks.test.ts > gateway server hooks > forwards hook sessionMode to isolated runner for direct and mapped agent hooks
  [gateway] starting HTTP server...
  [gateway] http server listening (0 plugins)

  ✓ |gateway-server| ../../src/gateway/server.hooks.test.ts > gateway server hooks > forwards hook sessionMode to isolated runner for direct and mapped agent hooks 7025ms
  ```

- **Persistent reuse proof:** The runner test exercises a hook-sourced run with `sessionMode: "persistent"` and asserts `resolveCronSession` is called with `forceNew: false`, the stable key `agent:default:hook:webhook:customer-42`, and the embedded agent receives the stored session id/key instead of a run-scoped key.

  ```console
  stdout | ../../src/cron/isolated-agent/run.session-key-isolation.test.ts > runCronIsolatedAgentTurn isolated session identity > allows explicit persistent hook runs to reuse the stored session
  [diagnostic] message queued: sessionId=persistent-hook-run-1 sessionKey=agent:default:hook:webhook:customer-42 source=cron-isolated queueDepth=1 sessionState=idle
  [diagnostic] message processed: channel=cron chatId=unknown messageId=unknown sessionId=persistent-hook-run-1 sessionKey=agent:default:hook:webhook:customer-42 outcome=completed duration=4ms

  ✓ |cron| ../../src/cron/isolated-agent/run.session-key-isolation.test.ts > runCronIsolatedAgentTurn isolated session identity > allows explicit persistent hook runs to reuse the stored session 87ms
  ```

- **Default isolated proof:** The runner tests exercise the default/omitted session mode and assert `forceNew: true`; repeated default isolated runs with the same logical session key produce distinct run-scoped session keys, preserving isolation.

  ```console
  stdout | ../../src/cron/isolated-agent/run.session-key-isolation.test.ts > runCronIsolatedAgentTurn isolated session identity > uses a run-scoped key for embedded isolated cron execution
  [diagnostic] message queued: sessionId=isolated-run-1 sessionKey=agent:default:cron:daily-monitor:run:isolated-run-1 source=cron-isolated queueDepth=1 sessionState=idle
  [diagnostic] message processed: channel=cron chatId=unknown messageId=unknown sessionId=isolated-run-1 sessionKey=agent:default:cron:daily-monitor:run:isolated-run-1 outcome=completed duration=244ms

  stdout | ../../src/cron/isolated-agent/run.session-key-isolation.test.ts > runCronIsolatedAgentTurn isolated session identity > keeps embedded isolated cron prompt-cache affinity stable across run sessions
  [diagnostic] message queued: sessionId=isolated-run-a sessionKey=agent:default:cron:daily-monitor:run:isolated-run-a source=cron-isolated queueDepth=1 sessionState=idle
  [diagnostic] message queued: sessionId=isolated-run-b sessionKey=agent:default:cron:daily-monitor:run:isolated-run-b source=cron-isolated queueDepth=1 sessionState=idle

  ✓ |cron| ../../src/cron/isolated-agent/run.session-key-isolation.test.ts > runCronIsolatedAgentTurn isolated session identity > uses a run-scoped key for embedded isolated cron execution 476ms
  ✓ |cron| ../../src/cron/isolated-agent/run.session-key-isolation.test.ts > runCronIsolatedAgentTurn isolated session identity > keeps embedded isolated cron prompt-cache affinity stable across run sessions 137ms
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** The proof run passed with the gateway dispatch test and both runner lifecycle paths covered.

  ```console
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
       Tests  4 passed | 27 skipped (31)
    Duration  15.90s
  ```

- **What was not tested:** This proof uses the real gateway HTTP dispatch path and runner/session plumbing, but mocks final model execution to keep the run deterministic and credential-free.


## Testing

Passed locally after the latest rebase/update:

- `node scripts/pre-commit/pnpm-audit-prod.mjs --audit-level=high && pnpm config:schema:check && pnpm config:docs:check && pnpm test src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/gateway/hooks-mapping.test.ts src/gateway/hooks.test.ts src/gateway/server.hooks.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.session-key-isolation.test.ts && pnpm build`
- `pnpm test src/gateway/hooks.test.ts src/gateway/hooks-mapping.test.ts src/gateway/server.hooks.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/session.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.session-key-isolation.test.ts`
- `pnpm test src/config/config-misc.test.ts`
- `pnpm config:docs:gen`
- `pnpm config:schema:check`
- `pnpm config:docs:check`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `pnpm build`

I did not run the full monorepo test suite; this is a large workspace, and the targeted acceptance tests plus changed-file gate/build cover the hook/config/cron surfaces touched here.

## Prior art

- Replaces/revives stale closed PR #27193 with a fresh current-main branch.
- Related issue: #11665.
target.author: ryanrhughes
